mechanism_narrative: A rising industrial production index signals expanding real output and capacity utilization, which drives corporate earnings growth and supports higher equity prices over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward S&P 500 return is negative in more than 50% of months when INDPRO increases relative to 3 months prior, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate falling below 6% signals that the labor market is tightening, which historically precedes strong consumer spending and equity gains, as wage pressures remain benign at this early stage of recovery.
failure_condition: If the average 60-day forward return following UNRATE crossing below 6% is less than or equal to zero, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in payroll employment and retail sales indicates robust consumer demand, which directly boosts corporate revenues and stock prices over the near term.
failure_condition: If in months where both PAYEMS and RSAFS increase year-over-year, the 20-day forward S&P 500 return is negative more than 40% of the time, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: With the federal funds rate at the zero lower bound, monetary policy is maximally accommodative, compressing discount rates and supporting equity valuations; this effect is regime-specific and reverses when rates exit the near-zero zone.
failure_condition: If the 5-day forward return is negative on more than 50% of days when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding 1 million units annually signals a sustained recovery in residential investment, which has strong positive spillovers to employment and consumer spending, driving equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: If the average 60-day forward return after HOUST exceeds 1000 is less than 1%, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: A rising real GDP level indicates overall economic growth, which directly translates into higher corporate profits and stock prices over a monthly horizon; the relationship is persistent across cycles.
failure_condition: If the correlation between GDPC1 quarterly changes and subsequent 20-day S&P 500 returns is less than 0.1 over any 5-year rolling window, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales rise but industrial production falls, it suggests an inventory buildup that will later depress production and profits, leading to negative equity returns over the very short term.
failure_condition: If the 5-day forward return is positive in more than 50% of weeks where RSAFS increases and INDPRO decreases, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: The consumer price index exceeding 235 (near the snapshot level) signals rising inflation that prompts expectations of tighter monetary policy, which negatively affects equity valuations through higher discount rates.
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward return is positive in more than 50% of instances following CPIAUCSL exceeding 235, the hypothesis is falsified.
